name: cover-subtitle
text: An end-to-end, role-based workflow from registration and pairing to race operations, official results, and rewards.
textPreview: An end-to-end, role-based workflow from registration and pairing to race operations, official results, and rewards.

text: React · Spring Boot · SQL Server · JWT
textPreview: React · Spring Boot · SQL Server · JWT

text:
EquiX is an SWP391 project that models the complete lifecycle of horse racing tournament management.
The product is not only a user interface; its main value is an integrated workflow across five business roles.
This presentation focuses on the architecture, V4 rules, result governance, and test evidence.

title: A race succeeds only when every role acts at the right time

name: slide-title
text: A race succeeds only when every role acts at the right time
textPreview: A race succeeds only when every role acts at the right time

text: When data is fragmented or permissions are unclear, a system may accept invalid registrations, start a race too early, allow prediction edits after lock, or publish unverified results.
textPreview: When data is fragmented or permissions are unclear, a system may accept invalid registrations, start a race too early, allow prediction e...

text:
• Incorrect authorization
• Lost data integrity
• Unverified results
textPreview: • Incorrect authorization | • Lost data integrity | • Unverified results

text: A single platform uses explicit business states, JWT-based API authorization, and a mandatory approval chain so every role acts in the correct sequence.
textPreview: A single platform uses explicit business states, JWT-based API authorization, and a mandatory approval chain so every role acts in the co...

text: Each role receives only required permissions
textPreview: Each role receives only required permissions

text: Every result becomes official through two steps
textPreview: Every result becomes official through two steps

text:
Open with the coordination problem instead of listing features.
Emphasize that the backend enforces the rules; the frontend reflects state and guides the user.

title: The platform covers the full race operations chain

name: slide-title
text: The platform covers the full race operations chain
textPreview: The platform covers the full race operations chain

name: slide-subtitle
text: Guests and five business roles have dedicated screens, permissions, and actions.
textPreview: Guests and five business roles have dedicated screens, permissions, and actions.

text: View races and leaderboard; create an account
textPreview: View races and leaderboard; create an account

textPreview: Create horses, invite jockeys, submit race pairs

text: Accept or reject invitations; view assignments
textPreview: Accept or reject invitations; view assignments

text: Run fitness checks, start, simulate, and report
textPreview: Run fitness checks, start, simulate, and report

text: Predict with points; claim and redeem rewards
textPreview: Predict with points; claim and redeem rewards

text: Approve accounts/pairs, create events, publish results
textPreview: Approve accounts/pairs, create events, publish results

text:
Referees have no public registration flow; only an Administrator can create a referee account.
Horse Owners and Jockeys require Administrator verification, while Spectators are activated immediately to reduce friction.

title: Three layers keep business rules independent of the UI

name: slide-title
text: Three layers keep business rules independent of the UI
textPreview: Three layers keep business rules independent of the UI

name: slide-subtitle
text: Every request passes through the API and authorization checks before SQL Server is updated.
textPreview: Every request passes through the API and authorization checks before SQL Server is updated.

text:
Role-based routing
Shared components
Real-time race tracking
textPreview: Role-based routing | Shared components | Real-time race tracking

text:
RBAC + JWT
Workflow services
Transaction validation
textPreview: RBAC + JWT | Workflow services | Transaction validation

text:
Spring Data JPA
Foreign keys + indexes
Transaction integrity
textPreview: Spring Data JPA | Foreign keys + indexes | Transaction integrity

text: Sign in → JWT carries user/role → Spring Security authenticates → Service validates rules → JPA commits transaction
textPreview: Sign in → JWT carries user/role → Spring Security authenticates → Service validates rules → JPA commits transaction

text:
JWT supports stateless REST; the backend never trusts a role supplied by the frontend.
SQL Server is the production runtime. H2 is used only by automated tests.

title: Five roles form a system of mutual control

name: slide-title
text: Five roles form a system of mutual control
textPreview: Five roles form a system of mutual control

text:
Own horse records
Invite jockeys
Register for races
textPreview: Own horse records | Invite jockeys | Register for races

text:
Decide invitations
Maintain pairings
Track performance
textPreview: Decide invitations | Maintain pairings | Track performance

text:
Run pre-race checks
Operate live races
Sign reports
textPreview: Run pre-race checks | Operate live races | Sign reports

text:
Watch races
Predict with points
Claim rewards
textPreview: Watch races | Predict with points | Claim rewards

text:
Create events and races
Approve data
Publish results
textPreview: Create events and races | Approve data | Publish results

text:
An Administrator cannot start a race; that permission belongs only to the assigned Referee.
This separation of responsibilities prevents one role from controlling the entire process.

title: A pairing can race only after six validation steps

name: slide-title
text: A pairing can race only after six validation steps
textPreview: A pairing can race only after six validation steps

name: slide-subtitle
text: The one-to-one constraint is stored as a pairing contract, not as temporary UI state.
textPreview: The one-to-one constraint is stored as a pairing contract, not as temporary UI state.

text:
Owner selects a race
and jockey
textPreview: Owner selects a race | and jockey

text: Core rule: one horse ↔ one jockey in an active pairing; registration is blocked until the pair exists.
textPreview: Core rule: one horse ↔ one jockey in an active pairing; registration is blocked until the pair exists.

text:
If registration is disabled, check the horse status, invitation, pairing, deadline, and Administrator approval in that order.
This workflow prevents incomplete race entries without a horse or jockey.

title: The race lifecycle is the backbone of the platform

name: slide-title
text: The race lifecycle is the backbone of the platform
textPreview: The race lifecycle is the backbone of the platform

name: slide-subtitle
text: Each state enables valid actions and locks operations that are no longer allowed.
textPreview: Each state enables valid actions and locks operations that are no longer allowed.

text:
Predictions lock when the race reaches STANDBY.
The Administrator manages registration states, but only the assigned Referee can start or complete a race.
Special branch: REPORT_READY ↔ REVISION_REQUIRED; a race that has not started may become CANCELLED.

title: V4 rules are enforced on the server, not only in the UI

name: slide-title
text: V4 rules are enforced on the server, not only in the UI
textPreview: V4 rules are enforced on the server, not only in the UI

text: The business document defines 25 hard rules. These five rules preserve the consistency of the demo workflow and address key review questions.
textPreview: The business document defines 25 hard rules. These five rules preserve the consistency of the demo workflow and address key review questi...

text:
The UI guides the user.
The server decides whether an action is valid.
The database preserves relationships and transactions.
textPreview: The UI guides the user. | The server decides whether an action is valid. | The database preserves relationships and transactions.

text: Registration closes one week before race time
textPreview: Registration closes one week before race time

text: Each spectator gets one prediction per race
textPreview: Each spectator gets one prediction per race

text: Only the assigned referee can start a race
textPreview: Only the assigned referee can start a race

text: Results require a report + Administrator approval
textPreview: Results require a report + Administrator approval

text:
Additional rules include: all races use Turf, the prize pool cannot be negative, referees cannot have schedule conflicts, and disqualification requires a reason.
Critical validations live in the service layer so direct API calls cannot bypass the UI.

title: Predictions engage spectators without real-money betting

name: slide-title
text: Predictions engage spectators without real-money betting
textPreview: Predictions engage spectators without real-money betting

name: slide-subtitle
text: Spectators use points; rewards are issued only after results become OFFICIAL.
textPreview: Spectators use points; rewards are issued only after results become OFFICIAL.

text:
Choose a horse–jockey pair
and place points before STANDBY
textPreview: Choose a horse–jockey pair | and place points before STANDBY

text:
Race reaches STANDBY
→ prediction cannot be edited
textPreview: Race reaches STANDBY | → prediction cannot be edited

text:
Administrator publishes OFFICIAL
→ settle points and issue rewards
textPreview: Administrator publishes OFFICIAL | → settle points and issue rewards

text:
Under V4, first place receives horse goods, second place a voucher, and third place a drink coupon.
The prize pool is display data allocated 60/30/10; it does not represent a real-money transaction.

title: Simulation starts only after the workflow is valid

name: slide-title
text: Simulation starts only after the workflow is valid
textPreview: Simulation starts only after the workflow is valid

text: The live race view synchronizes time and lane positions. Final positions remain provisional until the report is submitted and approved.
textPreview: The live race view synchronizes time and lane positions. Final positions remain provisional until the report is submitted and approved.

text: Predictions are locked before the start
textPreview: Predictions are locked before the start

alt: Live race track with 12 horses moving simultaneously

text:
For the demo, select a prepared race with enough FIT entries before moving it to STANDBY.
RaceTrack keeps horseId separate from registrationId so every horse receives the correct real-time update.

name: slide-subtitle
text: This chain prevents one role from both operating and publishing the entire race.
textPreview: This chain prevents one role from both operating and publishing the entire race.

text:
Complete simulation
Record incidents / DNF / DQ
Sign and submit report
textPreview: Complete simulation | Record incidents / DNF / DQ | Sign and submit report

text:
Review the report
Apply valid rank changes
Publish or request revision
textPreview: Review the report | Apply valid rank changes | Publish or request revision

text:
Update leaderboard
Allocate prizes 60/30/10
Settle predictions + rewards
textPreview: Update leaderboard | Allocate prizes 60/30/10 | Settle predictions + rewards

text: If the report fails review: REPORT_READY → REVISION_REQUIRED → referee revises and resubmits
textPreview: If the report fails review: REPORT_READY → REVISION_REQUIRED → referee revises and resubmits

text:
This is the most important separation of duties in the project.
Even a clean race requires a minimally signed report; there is no shortcut to OFFICIAL.

text:
Backend tests use the test profile and temporary H2 database; they do not replace the SQL Server runtime.
Frontend tests and the production build were run independently to validate logic and packaging.

Do not cancel the race used for the demo flow.
If a step already changed state, use another prepared race instead of editing SQL directly.
Back up the database before the presentation.

EquiX turns complex business logic
into a controlled workflow.
